name: Enter Settings menu
description: Hold the Shift button, press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Adjust brightness
description: Hold the Shift button, hold the Learn / Input button, turn the Vertical (Up/Down) encoder.

name: Pad refresh rate

spans.text: Changes the pad refresh rate, which can help when video recording the pads.

description: Hold the Clip button, turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Metronome

spans.text: Default metronome level is 50%.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Tap Tempo (Metronome) button.

name: Undo

spans.text: Note: If a preset is changed or you enter sound editor, then the undo history is cleared.

description: Press the Back / Undo (Redo) button.

name: Redo

spans.text: Note: If a preset is changed or you enter sound editor, then the redo history is cleared.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Back / Undo (Redo) button.

name: Qwerty keyboard

spans.text: When loading, saving, browsing or renaming, the deluge QWERTY keyboard can be used. It pops up automatically, except in song loading where the keyboard is hidden to enable viewing scrolling content, Tap grid to bring up keyboard.

description: Press the QWERTY keyboard overlay.

name: Change value in currently selected menu
description: Turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Fine tune decimal value in vertical menu

spans.text: To edit decimal values in the currently selected vertical menu, use the horizontal encoder to select the decimal position and turn select to edit the value at that position.

description: Turn the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Fine tune decimal value in horizontal menu

spans.text: Press and turn select or hold shift and turn select to edit fine edit decimal values in the currently selected horizontal menu.

description: Press the Select (Settings) encoder, turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Shift button, turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Check current zoom level
description: Press the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder.

name: Change current zoom level

spans.text: When you
clockwise, the resolution doubles. So instead of 16th notes, you'll see 32nd notes displayed.
Similarly, when you
counterclockwise, the resolution is halved.
Note #1: White notes in song and clip mode require zooming in to see exact position in the timeline.

The maximum zoom level has been increased. Now, the maximum zoom is the point the point where the entire timeline is represented by a single grid cell.
  - This allows for more flexibility when entering long notes and chord progressions.
  - While changing the zoom level, the horizontal encoder will briefly pause while passing the zoom level which represents the entire sequence. This is to prevent frustration from users who are used to the prior limitations.

description: Hold the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder, turn the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder.

name: Scroll horizontally (left or right)

spans.text: Scroll left and right across the grid.

description: Turn the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder.

name: Scroll vertically (up or down)

spans.text: Scroll up and down across the main grid or sidebar.

description: Turn the Vertical (Up/Down) encoder.

name: Navigate in Vertical Menu

spans.text: Turn select encoder to scroll up and down in a vertical menu.

description: Turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Navigate on Current Horizontal Menu Page

spans.text: With the current horizontal menu page open, press Synth, Kit, MIDI, or CV to select one of the four horizontal menu slots on the page.

description: Press the Synth button. Press the Kit button. Press the MIDI button. Press the CV button.

name: Navigate to the Previous or Next Horizontal Menu Page

spans.text:
Press scale to navigate to the previous horizontal menu page.
Press cross screen to navigate to the next horizontal menu page.

description: Press the Scale button. Press the Cross-screen button.

name: Navigate to the Previous or Next Adjacent Horizontal Menu

spans.text:
Press shift + scale to navigate to the previous adjacent horizontal menu.
Press shift + cross screen to navigate to the next adjacent horizontal menu.
Moves to the next adjacent horizontal menu.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Scale button. Hold the Shift button, press the Cross-screen button.

name: Enter / Step into the currently selected menu

spans.text: Press select encoder to enter the currently selected menu.

description: Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Navigate Backwards in Menu

spans.text: Go back up one level from within the menu / browser / file system.

description: Press the Back / Undo (Redo) button.

name: Return to Session or Arranger from Menu

spans.text: Exits a menu and returns to Session or Arranger View.

description: Press the Song button.

name: Layered Shortcuts

spans.text: Cycles through layered shortcuts when a shortcut pad has multiple actions assigned.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Grid pad (lit or unlit).

name: External controller to play synth or kit

spans.text: In Synth, any audition / row pressed assigns all. In Kit, only the pressed instrument / row is learned. Deluge learns incoming MIDI note data and maps it to the clip.

description: Hold the Learn / Input button, hold the Audition / Section button for row, trigger the external MIDI controller.

name: External controller to trigger clip (from song mode)

spans.text: Can trigger clip mutes from Song mode via an external MIDI controller. Deluge uses MIDI notes, not CC values, for mapping.

description: Hold the Learn / Input button, hold the Audition / Section button for row, trigger the external MIDI controller.

name: External controller to trigger play (from song mode)

spans.text: Can trigger Play from Song mode via an external MIDI controller. Deluge uses MIDI notes, not CC values, for mapping.

description: Hold the Learn / Input button, hold the Play button, trigger the external MIDI controller.

name: External controller to trigger record (from song mode)

spans.text: Can trigger Record from Song mode via an external MIDI controller. Deluge uses MIDI notes, not CC values, for mapping.

description: Hold the Learn / Input button, hold the Record button, trigger the external MIDI controller.

name: Unlearn external controller to play synth or kit

spans.text: Any already learned MIDI functions flash when the Learn/Input button alone is held.

description: Hold the Shift button, hold the Learn / Input button, press the Audition / Section button for row.

name: Unlearn external controller to trigger clip (from song mode)

name: Unlearn external controller to trigger play (from song mode)
description: Hold the Shift button, hold the Learn / Input button, hold the Play button.

name: Unlearn external controller to trigger record (from song mode)
description: Hold the Shift button, hold the Learn / Input button, hold the Record button.

name: External control of parameter - first select parameter

spans.text: Learns external control of the selected Deluge parameter.

description: Hold the Learn / Input button, trigger the external MIDI controller.

name: Unlearn external control of parameter - first select parameter
description: Hold the Shift button, hold the Learn / Input button.

name: Mute by external MIDI for individual kit rows - in kit mode

spans.text: Learns an external MIDI note to mute individual kit instruments / rows.

description: Hold the Learn / Input button, hold the Mute / Launch button for row, trigger the external MIDI controller.

name: MIDI Follow Unlearn

spans.text: Unlearns a MIDI Follow channel or device from the current MIDI Follow submenu.

description: Hold the Shift button, hold the Learn / Input button.

name: New song

spans.text: Creates a new song.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Load (New) button.

name: Delete song

spans.text: Scroll to the song to delete, then use Shift and Save/Delete to delete it. Press Save/Delete again to confirm.

description: Press the Load (New) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Shift button, press the Save (Delete) button. Press the Save (Delete) button.

name: Load song (use saved tempo)

spans.text: Note #1: Hold SHIFT and turn SELECT to fast-scroll songs on the display.

description: Press the Load (New) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Load (New) button.

name: Load song (keep current tempo)

spans.text: If you wish to maintain the tempo of the old song into the new one, press down on the tempo knob while you press the load button. And if tempo magnitude matching is enabled, then a multiple of the old song’s tempo may be used if it means a less drastic change to the new song’s tempo.
Or, if the Deluge is playing synced as a slave, the tempo will remain the same regardless, with the tempo magnitude matching setting taking effect if enabled.

description: Press the Load (New) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Tempo (Swing) encoder. Press the Load (New) button.

name: Load song (delay song change)

spans.text: As your new song may take up to a few seconds to load if it contains a lot of samples, there is a way to more precisely specify its launch-time, too. After selecting the song you wish to switch to, hold down the load button rather than pressing it momentarily. While holding the button down, the Deluge will load the song, indicating “DONE” when loading is complete. Then only when you release your press on the load button will the Deluge become armed to switch the song at the completion of the current loop.

description: Press the Load (New) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Load (New) button.

name: Fast scroll song list
description: Press the Load (New) button. Hold the Shift button, turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Save song

spans.text: Note: Song slots with a '.' after the name already exist, and 'OVERwrite' will flash when trying to save. A, B, C designate iterations of the same song.

description: Press the Save (Delete) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Save song, collect all samples

spans.text: Saves the song, creates a folder with the same name, and copies all used sample files into it under /SONGS.

description: Press the Save (Delete) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Change tempo (coarse)

spans.text:
Official: Turn the tempo encoder to change tempo by approximately +/- 4 increments.

Community (default): Turn the tempo encoder to change tempo by +/-1 increments.

Note: You can change the default behaviour in the community firmware.

description: Turn the Tempo (Swing) encoder.

name: Change tempo (fine)

spans.text:
Official: Press and turn the tempo encoder to change tempo by +/- 1 increments.

Community (default): Press and turn the tempo encoder to change tempo by approximately +/- 4 increments.

Note: You can change the default behaviour in the community firmware.

description: Hold the Tempo (Swing) encoder, turn the Tempo (Swing) encoder.

name: Change tempo (secret power up)

spans.text: Use this to increase tempo from 0 to FAST, FAST!

description: Hold the Learn / Input button, turn the Tempo (Swing) encoder.

name: Grab tempo from existing audio clip
description: Hold the Tempo (Swing) encoder, press the Lit grid pad.

name: Nudge MIDI clock
description: Hold the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder, turn the Tempo (Swing) encoder.

name: Sync scaling for unusual time signitures

spans.text: Sync-scaling is tied to the length of one clip in a song, and tells the Deluge that that clip’s length should be squeezed into 1 bar of incoming MIDI beat clock (or 2 bars, or 4 or 8 bars, depending on how long the clip is; the Deluge will use whatever magnitude of sync-scaling causes the smallest change in tempo).

To enable sync-scaling, enter clip view for the clip that you wish to tie sync-scaling to. Press the sync-scaling button to set this clip as the sync-scaling clip. The button will blink continuously, indicating that this is the sync-scaling clip. If you leave clip view for this clip, the button will remain illuminated, but will cease to blink, indicating that sync-scaling is active, but not for this clip. Sync-scaling may be switched off at any time by pressing the sync-scaling button.

Even while the Deluge is playing synced as a slave, sync-scaling may be switched on or off, and the sync-scaling clip may have its length changed. Despite any such changes, the Deluge will keep the sync-scaling clip playing in time to the syncing master. It will also attempt to keep all other clips in time; this works best if the other clips are of the same time signature as the sync-scaling clip (that is, their lengths are the same, or half our double, or 4 times shorter or longer, etc.)

description: Press the Sync-Scaling button.

name: Swing

spans.text: Swing interval is 1/16th notes by default, but can be adjusted in the settings menu.

description: Hold the Shift button, turn the Tempo (Swing) encoder.

name: Enter Performance View

spans.text: Opens Performance View from Session or Arranger View when tapping the keyboard button. The short press time is configurable in the settings menu under "hold press time."

description: Press the Performance button (keyboard icon).

name: Enter Performance View (Temporarily)

spans.text: Opens Performance View Temporarily from Session or Arranger View while holding the keyboard button and switches back to Session or Arranger View after releasing the keyboard button.

description: Hold the Performance button (keyboard icon).

name: Performance View Editing Mode

spans.text: Opens the value and parameter editing modes for Performance View.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Performance button (keyboard icon).

name: Save Performance View Changes

spans.text: Saves edited Performance View values.

description: Press the Save (Delete) button, press the Performance button (keyboard icon).

name: Reload Performance View Changes

spans.text: Reloads the saved Performance View layout.

description: Press the Load (New) button, press the Performance button (keyboard icon).

name: Clear Performance View Held Values

spans.text: Clears held FX values in Performance View.

description: Hold the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder, press the Back / Undo (Redo) button.

name: Master Compressor Mode

spans.text: When Affect Entire is on and Sidechain is enabled, this adjusts the master compressor mode.

description: Press the Affect Entire button. Turn the upper parameter encoder.

name: VU Meter

spans.text: Press the LEVEL/PAN BUTTON while it is already selected to show the VU meter.

description: Press the Affect Entire button. Press the LEVEL/PAN BUTTON.

name: Add clip into arranger

name: Change / select instance's clip

spans.text: This command is fundamental to arranger principles. You can switch between Arranger only clips (white) or Clips assigned to different Sections in Session View.

spans.text: The fully lit left-most grid button deletes the instance from arranger only. This can be undone and redone.

description: Press the Mute / Launch button for row.

spans.text: Play switches from clip loop mode back to arranger at the left-most visible column. The change happens at the end of the loop, in time with the sequence.

description: Press the Mute / Launch button for row.

Changes INFInite to a repeat count for the section. The display counts down; press Select to cancel the section switch during countdown.

name: Change audio output mode to enable/disable monitoring and looping functionality in session view

spans.text:
The community firmware changes how input monitoring is set for audio tracks.

Player: monitoring always off

Sampler: if no recording exists or you are currently recording, monitoring will be on

Sampler: if recording exists, monitoring will be off

Looper: monitoring is always on and overdub recording is used

description: Press the Grid pad (lit or unlit). Turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Create new audio track in arranger view

spans.text: Creates a new audio track from Arranger view.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Change audio output mode to enable/disable monitoring and looping functionality in arranger view

spans.text:
The community firmware changes how input monitoring is set for audio tracks.

Player: monitoring always off

Sampler: if no recording exists or you are currently recording, monitoring will be on

Sampler: if recording exists, monitoring will be off

Looper: monitoring is always on and overdub recording is used

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

spans.text: Must be in Clip mode.

spans.text: Uses the waveform view to change the audio clip start or end point.

name: Adjust length - audio clip waveform

spans.text: The clip remains time-stretched, and shortened clips in waveform view play at a slower speed to fit the same time window. In many cases it may be better to record samples instead. Then you may need to use the tempo-from-audio shortcut afterward to apply the original tempo again.

description: Hold the Shift button, turn the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder.

name: Set clip length to sample length

spans.text:
This is a community firmware addition

Also available in Audio Clip Sound Menu > ACTIONS.

description: Press the Vertical (Up/Down) encoder, press the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder.

name: Adjust audio clip length without timestretching

spans.text: This is a community firmware addition

name: Create new synth
description: Hold the Shift button, press the Synth button.

name: View synth preset browser
description: Hold the Load (New) button, press the Synth button.

name: Change synth preset

spans.text: Changes the current synth preset.

description: Turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Clone synth preset

spans.text: A single preset can only appear in one active clip, so clone the original preset for multiple instances in the same song. It is good practice to clone first when tweaking to avoid affecting interdependent songs.

description: Hold the Load (New) button, press the Synth button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Load (New) button, select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Save synth preset
description: Hold the Save (Delete) button, press the Synth button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Create DX7 Synth

spans.text: With the DX7 community feature enabled, this creates a new DX7 synth.

description: Hold the STUTTER/CUSTOM 1 BUTTON, press the Synth button.

name: Chromatic sample - same sample, different pitches (on new synth)

spans.text: Press Select once to load as a chromatic sample: the same sample is used at different pitches.
Deluge detects the pitch of provided samples regardless of filename, though for multi-samples it is best to order samples on the SD card from low to high where possible.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Load (New) button. Press the Select (Settings) encoder. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Basic sample - load sample with no pitch detection

spans.text: Hold Select and turn to choose BASIc, which loads a sample with no pitch detection.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Load (New) button. Press the Select (Settings) encoder. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Select (Settings) encoder, select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Multi-sampling

spans.text: Scroll through folders to select a sample or parent folder for multi-samples. Hold Select and turn to choose MULTi for multi-sampling.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Load (New) button. Press the Select (Settings) encoder. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Select (Settings) encoder, select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Single cycle waveforms

spans.text: Single-cycle waveforms should be under 20ms. Deluge automatically transposes to C and sets loop mode. Use the SINGle option to force samples into this mode.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Load (New) button. Press the Select (Settings) encoder. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Select (Settings) encoder, select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: External sound source

spans.text: Use an external input as an oscillator source by selecting IN. You can pitch-shift around the source or play chords with several sequenced notes at once. A stereo-to-mono adapter can feed one source to OSC1 INL and another to OSC2 INR.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Grid pad (lit or unlit). Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Play button.

name: Setup MIDI sequencing of notes

spans.text: Select MIDI channel 1-16 for note output.

description: Press the MIDI button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: MIDI sequencing of parameters - first select parameter

spans.text: Deluge labels do not apply. Use any button to map the function. 'None' means nothing is assigned.

description: Hold the parameter encoders, turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Turn the parameter encoders.

name: Change dial control, but keep automation

spans.text: Parameters with recorded automation are not shown with the normal Change MIDI Parameter command, so this command avoids writing automation in error.

description: Hold the parameter encoders, hold the Select (Settings) encoder, turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Load MIDI instrument preset

spans.text: Loads a MIDI instrument preset for the current MIDI clip.

description: Hold the Load (New) button, press the MIDI button.

name: Save MIDI instrument preset

spans.text: Saves the current MIDI clip as a MIDI instrument preset.

description: Hold the Save (Delete) button, press the MIDI button.

name: Save MIDI CC Labels to Song

spans.text: Saves renamed MIDI CC labels into the current song.

description: Press the Save (Delete) button.

name: Save MIDI CC Labels to Preset

spans.text: Saves renamed MIDI CC labels into the current MIDI preset.

description: Press the Save (Delete) button, press the MIDI button.

name: Save MIDI CC Labels to File

spans.text: Writes the current MIDI CC labels to a MIDI device definition file.

description: Press the Save (Delete) button. Turn the parameter encoders.

name: Load MIDI CC Labels from File

spans.text: Loads MIDI CC labels from a MIDI device definition file.

description: Press the Load (New) button. Turn the parameter encoders.

name: Unlink MIDI Device Definition

spans.text: Used on the File Linked setting to unlink the current MIDI device definition file.

description: Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Cycle default scales
description: Hold the Shift button, press the Scale button.

name: Change root note

spans.text: Sets the new root note. This can also be set from keyboard view.

description: Hold the Scale button, press the Audition / Section button for row.

name: Alter current scale

spans.text: Sharpens or flattens a note. Scale must be lit; otherwise all notes / semitones are present. Prepend Shift to do this silently.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Create new kit
description: Hold the Shift button, press the Kit button.

name: View kit preset browser
description: Hold the Load (New) button, press the Kit button.

name: Change kit preset

spans.text: Changes the current kit preset.

description: Turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Save kit preset
description: Hold the Save (Delete) button, press the Kit button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Clone kit preset

spans.text: A single preset can only appear in one active clip, so clone the original preset for multiple instances in the same song. It is good practice to clone first when tweaking to avoid affecting interdependent songs.

description: Hold the Load (New) button, press the Kit button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Load (New) button, select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Load sample into a kit row

spans.text: Scroll to a sample and press Select. Shift + Browse/Grid shortcut on an existing row can also open the browser.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Load (New) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Load sample into a kit row silently
description: Hold the Shift button, hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Load (New) button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Load all samples from folder to kit

spans.text: Scroll to the parent folder of the samples you want, or to a sample within the folder, then choose ALL.
Beware of CPU and memory overhead when loading large amounts of samples into kits. Keep KIT files minimal for best performance. This does not apply to multi-sampled synths.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Kit button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Select (Settings) encoder. Select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Slice sample

spans.text: Scroll to a sample, choose SLICE, then select the number of slices from 2-256.
You can add more samples to sliced kits, but not more slices to existing kits. Only one sliced sample per kit is possible.

description: Hold the Shift button. Press the Kit button. Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Hold the Select (Settings) encoder. Select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder. Select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Setting up choke group

spans.text: Select CHOKe. When playing, this stops all other notes in the same kit that are set to the same choke group.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Grid pad (lit or unlit). Turn the Select (Settings) encoder. Select an option from the on-screen menu. Press the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Rename kit row or instrument
description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the 'Name' grid pad.

name: Delete kit row

spans.text: Hold the audition pad for the row you want to delete, then press Save/Delete.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Save (Delete) button.

name: MIDI note output in a kit clip

spans.text: Multiple MIDI channels or notes can be set on each row of a kit.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the MIDI button.

name: MIDI note output in a kit clip: MIDI channel assign
description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, turn the lower parameter encoder.

name: MIDI note output in a kit clip: MIDI note value

name: Randomize drum sample (selected drum)

name: Randomize drum sample (multiple selected)

name: Delete unused kit rows
description: Hold the Kit button, hold the Shift button, press the Save (Delete) button.

name: Manual Slicing (Lazy Chop)

spans.text: While in Slice Menu.
Lazy chop allows the grid pads to manually chop the sample into slices. Press the green pad to start and lower pads to capture slices on the fly.

description: Press the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder.

name: Kit clip affect all - first select parameter

spans.text: Applies to all rows in a kit.

spans.text: The row is selected and the instrument or sample name flashes on the LCD, but no note sounds. You can then modify parameters, change presets, and so on.

name: Clear notes / audio file and automation

spans.text: Duplicates the clip content, doubles the clip length, and appends the duplicate.

spans.text: Multiple notes can be selected. New notes default to the last velocity setting. Default velocity is 64, with a range of 0-127.

spans.text: Turn counter-clockwise to adjust probability. Turn clockwise to adjust iteration.

name: Note probability group

spans.text: When probability is the same, all notes follow the action of the first note in the sequence. A '.' signifies that a probability group exists.

spans.text: Paste applies to all notes in the vertical columns at the current zoom level, including notes not visible above and below the grid.

spans.text: Cross-screen mode is linked to zoom level. Turn it on or off if you want to apply changes at different zoom levels.

description: Press the Cross-screen button.

name: Add instrument / row to kit

spans.text: Scroll to a sample. Press Back to go up one folder level.

spans.text: Loads a pattern without scaling the original file.

spans.text: Opens Automation View from Arranger View or any clip view.

description: Press the Clip button.

description: Press the Clip button.

spans.text: Returns to Automation Overview from Automation Editor or Note Velocity Editor.

name: Clear Automation in Automation View

spans.text: Clears all automations in the selected context

description: Hold the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder, press the Back / Undo (Redo) button.

spans.text: Toggles automatic scrolling during playback in Automation Arranger View.

description: Press the Cross-screen button.

name: Toggle Automation View Interpolation On or Off

spans.text: Toggles interpolation in Automation View.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Lit grid pad.

name: Toggle Automation View Pad Selection Mode

spans.text: Toggles pad selection mode in Automation View.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Unlit grid pad.

name: Enter Step Automation (with gold knobs)

spans.text: Hold note / step and turn gold knob(s) to adjust automation at the selected step

description: Hold the Grid pad (lit or unlit), turn the parameter encoders.

name: Enter Step Automation (with external midi)

spans.text: Hold note / step and send external midi cc to adjust automation at the selected step for the learned parameter(s)

description: Hold the Grid pad (lit or unlit), turn the external MIDI controller.

name: Enter Step Automation with Automation View Pad Selection Mode

spans.text: Press grid pad(s) to select step or range of steps and turn gold knobs to adjust step automation

description: Press the Grid pad (lit or unlit). Turn the parameter encoders.

name: Resample / record all audio output (begin instantly)

spans.text: Pressing shift+record begins output-recording instantly.
Records or resamples all audio output. Press Record again to stop.

The Deluge can record its audio output to a file on the SD card. This is useful both as a sonic tool, where the user may create interesting sounds on the Deluge and “resample” them for further use, or to allow the user to make high-quality recordings of compositions or performances.
This is not to be confused, though, with live looping, one use of which allows the Deluge’s output to be recorded into an audio clip.

Recorded WAV files will appear in the SAMPLES/RESAMPLE folder of the SD card. Also, after you've made a recording in this way, the next time you are taken to the file browser, you will be defaulted to your new file, allowing you to easily add it to a kit or as an audio clip.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Record button.

name: Resample / record all audio output (begin with playback)

spans.text: To begin output-recording at the exact same instant that playback is begun, press record+play.

description: Hold the Record button, press the Play button.

name: Loop resample / record all audio output (begin with playback, stop at end of the loop)

spans.text: Repeat Record + Play before the end of the loop. If already playing, hold Record + Play to quantize recording to the loop start.

description: Hold the Record button, press the Play button. Hold the Record button, press the Play button.

name: Resample / record all audio output (begin with voice)

spans.text: To begin output-recording at the exact same instant that a voice is sounded, hold down record and press an audition pad (or one of the main pads if you're in keyboard view).
Press Record to stop.

description: Hold the Record button, press the Audition / Section button for row.

name: Record sample into a new kit

spans.text: Better for offline recording than using audio clips or live looper recording.

Create a new, blank kit clip by pressing shift+kit (provided you were in clip view already). A new kit is created, and the user is placed in the file browser. However, to record a new sound rather than select a file, simply press the record button at this stage. The sound recorder is entered, and recording begins immediately, indicated by the word “REC” blinking on the Deluge’s display.
Press the record button again to end recording.
Note: After recording samples into a kit, the Deluge digitally normalizes the volume level of the recorded sample to make it as loud as possible without clipping.

description: Hold the Shift button, press the Kit button. Press the Record button.

name: Record sample into an existing kit

spans.text: To record further samples into an existing kit, hold down the “audition” pad on an unoccupied row, and press the record button. The sound recorder will be re-entered, and an additional sample may be recorded. This process may be repeated as many times as desired.
The sound recorder may also be entered via the sound editor for a particular sound.

description: Hold the Audition / Section button for row, press the Record button.

name: Audio Export

spans.text: Starts audio export from Session/Arranger, or Kit drum export when you are in Kit Instrument Clip View.

description: Press the Save (Delete) button, press the Record button.

name: Record external notes

spans.text: Once learned, the clip does not need to be visible to record incoming external notes into it.

description: Press the Record button. Press the Play button. Trigger the external MIDI controller.

name: Record live - notes into sequencer
description: Press the Play button. Press the Record button.

name: Record automation of parameter

spans.text: Records automation for the chosen parameter while playback is running.

description: Press the Record button. Press the Play button. Turn the parameter encoders.

name: Record MIDI automation

spans.text: CC with a '.' shown on the LCD indicates automation is already recorded.

description: Press the Record button. Press the Play button. Turn the parameter encoders.

name: Record MIDI step automation

spans.text: Records MIDI automation per step.

description: Hold the Grid pad (lit or unlit), turn the parameter encoders.

name: Record to arranger - live record parameter changes and MIDI notes (RECORD to end)

spans.text: Both Record and Song buttons flash while active. Play or Song stops recording. While this mode is active you cannot change to Clip or Arranger mode.
Live recording can capture parameter changes, MIDI notes, and parameter changes on existing MIDI-learned clips.

description: Hold the Record button, press the Song button.

name: Append recording to existing arrangement (from arrangement mode; RECORD to end)

spans.text: Move the play bar to the point where you want to append new live recording to the arranger. Everything to the right of the play bar is deleted and the new recording is appended. You can undo if a mistake is made.

description: Turn the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder. Press the Song button. Hold the Record button, press the Song button.

name: Toggle record arm
description: Hold the Record button, press the Mute / Launch button for row.

name: Start loop record
description: Press the Record button. Press the Play button.

name: Close loop recording - on recording clip row
description: Press the Mute / Launch button for row.

name: Close loop recording and immediately play in solo
description: Hold the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder, press the Mute / Launch button for row.

name: Record loop during playback
description: Press the Record button. Press the Play button. Press the Mute / Launch button for row.

name: Record overdub - press grid directly underneath existing audio clip
description: Press the Record button. Press the Play button. Hold the Record button, press the Grid pad (lit or unlit).

name: Record overdub - with continuous layering - press grid directly underneath existing audio clip
description: Press the Record button. Press the Play button. Hold the Record button, press the Audition / Section button for row.

name: Tempo detection for loop pedal recording - start with a blank song
description: Hold the Shift button, press the Tap Tempo (Metronome) button.

name: Preset tempo for loop recording

name: Enable count-in before audio recording

description: Press the Audition / Section button for row.

name: Enter Sound Editor menu

description: Hold the Key button (keyboard icon), turn the Select (Settings) encoder.

name: Chord Keyboard Row / Column Mode

description: Hold the Shift button, turn the Horizontal (Left/Right) encoder.

In the community firmware, turning a gold dial will display the name of the parameter it controls in addition to the current value on the OLED display.

description: Turn the parameter encoders.

spans.text: In the community firmware, pressing the circular buttons between the gold dials will display the parameters that each gold dial controls.

name: Waveform edit view - change start / end / loop
description: Hold the Shift button, press the Grid pad (lit or unlit).

spans.text: Click anywhere on the green start bar until it flashes, then click the target column to move it.

spans.text: Hold the green start bar and click on the grid to the right to create the loop start point. The loop start bar can then be moved like the start / end bars.

description: Hold the green grid column representing waveform start, press the Grid pad (lit or unlit).

name: Delete loop start point

spans.text: Hold anywhere on the blue loop-start bar and press the green start bar. The loop point disappears.

description: Hold the blue grid column representing waveform loop start, press the green grid column representing waveform start.

name: Create loop end point

spans.text: Hold the red end bar and click on the grid to the left to create the loop end point. The loop end bar can then be moved like the start / end bars.

description: Hold the red grid column representing waveform end, press the Grid pad (lit or unlit).

name: Delete loop end point

spans.text: Hold anywhere on the purple loop-end bar and press the red end bar. The loop point disappears.

description: Hold the purple grid column representing waveform loop end, press the red grid column representing waveform end.

name: Waveform Loop Lock

spans.text: Locks loop points together. Repeat to unlock.

description: Hold the blue grid column representing waveform loop start, press the purple grid column representing waveform loop end.
